text.title: Code Decomposition Principles Core Rules
text.summary: 5 hard rules every decomposition audit must obey.

rule.statement: A file is a candidate only if LOC ≥ 300 OR token count ≥ 10000.
rule.rationale: Below threshold, decomposition costs more than it saves; LLM-context fit is not pressured.

rule.statement: Mark a file as multi-responsibility iff ≥2 distinct concern tags (io, ui, logic, types, config) coexist in >10% of symbols each.
rule.rationale: Single-percentage threshold prevents trivial-helper mis-flags; the >10% floor anchors the signal.

rule.statement: High churn means >20 commits in the last 90 days AND ≥40% of commits touch a sibling file in a different concern bucket.

rationale.source: Adam Tornhill — Software Design X-Rays; CodeScene research

rule.rationale: Co-edit churn is the strongest signal that responsibilities are intertwined; raw commit count alone over-flags hot files.

rule.statement: Files above 10k tokens force agent context-saturation; emit a split recommendation even if SRP signal is weak.
rule.rationale: LLM-readable code matters as much as human-readable; ≥10k tokens blocks single-shot agent operations.

rule.statement: Every decision record names exactly one accountable owner; team / we / us is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership; future readers need a name to chase.
